triviaPool: Did you know? Guava leaves are rich in antioxidants, vitamin C, and flavonoids which contribute to their strong healing properties.
Oregano gets its name from the Greek words 'oros' (mountain) and 'ganos' (joy), meaning 'joy of the mountain'.
Boiling guava leaves for 15 minutes creates a powerful decoction traditionally used to treat diarrhea and stomach aches.
Philippine oregano (Mexican Mint) has thick, fuzzy leaves that contain high amounts of essential oils, making it excellent for cough relief.
Chewing tender guava leaves is a traditional folk remedy for bleeding gums and bad breath.
Oregano essential oil contains carvacrol and thymol, two compounds with potent antibacterial and antifungal properties.
The high vitamin C content in guava leaves helps boost the immune system and aids in the body's natural healing process.
